pages.raw_text:
The withdrawal of the Pressmen's
union from the Central Labor union
was announced last night. The
union withdrew as protest against
the central body refusing a seat to
Rev. w. J. Hindley as a fraternal
delegate. The withdrawal is made
to place the matter squarely before
the American Federation of Labor.
The communication stated that
President Gompers should insist on
the clergy being seated in central
bodies in view of the fact that he
had extended an invitation to them
to investigate labor from labor's
standpoint and had recommended
that seats be given the ministry in
labor organizations.


A delegate from the Bartenders'
union stated that this organization
would not aid the printing trades in
any way in the future and that the
bartenders' stock Of union pastors,
provided by the allied printing
trades to be placed on printed mat
ter, had been thrown in the waste
basket. The action of the bartend-
ers was taken because the printers
and pressmen insisted on the seat
ing of Rev. Hindley in the Central
Labor union.


Delegates from the newly organ
lied Laundry Workers union were
seated amid cheers, the fact that an
organization has been effected in
the teeth of the laundry trust being
recognized as great victory for la
Dor. f. A. Cooney was rendered a
vote of thanks by the central body,
and formal denial was made that
Mayor Daggett or the city hall ma
chine had anything to do with the
matter. the organizer having acted
under instruction given him several
months ago by the central body.


Resolutions were adopted de-
nouncing the statements of Mr..
Doust wherein he declares that the
new union has been organized for
political purposes as false.
